name: JL Trahan
subject: Tax should only be in commerce...NEVER based on your "owned property" each and every year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Texas Vote Now as a public service to all voters | 3355 West Alabama suite 980-ZIMMERN Houston Texas 77098
body:
Taxing peoples owned property annually is intellectually lazy. Tax belongs in commerce, NOT assessed annually against your owned property at the poin
| | [](https://email.cloud.secureclick.net/c/33448?id=1953783.750.1.80e981efb8610e2762447e2542b748a3)
---
[](https://email.cloud.secureclick.net/c/33448?id=1953783.751.1.8c7118d2473e921d0ddbb99f6985e79c) |
---
## Taxing peoples owned property annually is intellectually lazy.
---
|
---
# Tax belongs in commerce, NOT assessed annually against your owned property at the point of forfeiture in the courts.
It is not in ANY way Constitutional to tax a citizen annually for the right to own the assets for which they already have purchased and paid tax. ALL tax belongs in commerce. ALL OF IT. So...how did the entire tax burden of the state and nation end up on the homeowners, wage owners and small business? Lobbyists. That's how. Big Corps hired lobbyists to lobby and buy off your elected officials to put the entire tax burden on the wage earning, home owners and small business owners. Big Corps pay almost NO property tax and little income tax. In fact, they lobbied YOUR elected officials to FUND LOBBYISTS with YOUR TAX DOLLARS to then lobby your elected officials to RAISE YOUR TAXES. Are you sick of this yet? I bet you are and so are we. We can only fix this if we work together. That is why we started BIZPAC.us 7 years ago and you should be a part of it. I invite you to join us Thursday February 13th for a BIZPAC Roundtable Luncheon you will not forget. Learn what we are doing to stop this madness in Texas and more here: **https://gem.godaddy.com/p/ec1ccb1** Sincerely, JL Trahan
BIZPAC.us Founder and Executive Chairman
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud.secureclick.net/c/33448?id=1953783.752.1.b899ab1b6c6f12ea01e7348dfc42ab87) choose your membership level here:
---
Our state and federal legislators have targeted the small business owner with the bulk of the tax burden and regulatory. Their lobbyists have big corps paying almost zero tax and have turned their regulatory into secondary markets where they trade "credits" for cash and stick us with the bill. At BIZPAC, we have organized and are fighting back. Join with us and take control. Together, we are taking control and turning this around for small business owners.
---
## Announcing the VIP Founders Club
---
Join at the VIP Founders Club level and you and your guest never wait in line, pay a fee and you are invited to private briefings and exclusive receptions with only fellow VIP Founders Club Members. **We have 24 Luncheons and Receptions planned for 2025** **PLUS Special Events and trips to the Capital to work with our Legislators** **And** **the BIZPAC Center for Business Excellence Accelerator and PITCH ROOM**
---
|
---
| | Caption goes here
---
Caption goes here
---
| | [ Select your BIZPAC Membership ](https://email.cloud.secureclick.net/c/33448?id=1953783.753.1.cd2cb4856a3786aeea703dbb1c8dc371)
---
**It is time to renew your membership and we hope you will take advantage of this amazing offer.** **The small business owner is vulnerable alone, but, together we are strong.** **We are building an effective and amazing organization that fosters our business growth and guides our legislative bodies.** **-JL Trahan,**
**Founder & Executive Chairman**
---
---

name: Donald J. Trump
subject: I need to know…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc

Friend - Democrats are gearing up for an all-out fight to take back the Senate in 2026. They're already recruiting radical candidates and setting their sights on battleground states. If conservatives lose the Senate, the Left will ram through their Big Government tax-and-spend agenda, undoing _everything_ we've fought for. _We cannot let that happen._ **That 's why Club for Growth Action is already preparing to go on the offense.** We're building the war chest right now to ensure we keep a pro-growth, pro-freedom majority in the Senate. clean_body: I’m coming to you for backup Friend, it's Navy SEAL and U.S. Senator Tim Sheehy. I'm new to the Senate, but I'm no stranger to a tough fight. When it comes to confirming President Trump's team, a tough fight is what we're getting from Democrats. They're doing everything they can to obstruct the process. But Senate Republicans were elected to win tough fights. If that means working nights, weekends, and holidays, so be it. Friend, will you stand with Senate Republicans and me? Thank you, U.S. Senator Tim Sheehy (R-MT) ******************************************************************************************************** Tim Sheehy is a former Navy SEAL. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense. | Paid for by the NRSC. body:
| Peter, In a tough year for Democrats, your support was critical to helping Jonathan Nez VASTLY overperform his expected performance. That's why, with so much gloomy news floating around, I'm reaching out with good news. **In AZ-02, Nez performed better than both Kamala Harris and Ruben Gallego**. Considering all congressional races in 2024, Nez ranked 6th in Democratic overperformance, **outrunning Harris by 3.5 points and newly elected Senator Gallego by 0.6 points in his district.** While we all wanted to win, as a valued stakeholder who made our campaign possible, I want you to be among the first to see our post-election memo that explains exactly how remarkable the campaign you helped build was. name: Scalise, Steve
subject: VERY few people are paying attention to this…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
I won’t stand around while a bunch of New York Democrats are trying to hamstring President Trump's entire legislative agenda.
And it could endanger President Trump’s entire legislative agenda!
| |
---
| | | | [](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=9e2d4c8548264bd8bc10f23060d5ac6d&_e=Uhx8Y3mSo_q1xFSEZUJ_8hFHOgjIp9V4k6VGXqa3EshkhjVgab4IkKhVaLkDphwe1KnmUJP_yXTAk3dXwlnQX1qwwfhtRFqTXypH1AjeGxlR7taY2-_OmOeMIHRJBQOWHcmn95qY4SBiCRSczXve7RDW71l3d0uq6PitAaw1FjFitz9-AWwubGYt7VHtfE9_JzM64OLYgNH2dwWJkcl3Qst5M9kl590w_OoTcSj5D9MFSdO2omlh_5R6zTmyC25zMXh9_c3y8Zi10qfgEg_l2tVs3daQdTS-vS-Eca4yBUCWI460L9S1LbDr1ZcO76XH4PaSkqqINz-WrWKgS8Zs2xVnUau-5HYMRNEcyLwVXDonzjZH3qbNqEwkVcrcal1Jsb6pZW7HO6i-dP-iPmXdlA%3D%3D "Majority Leader Steve Scalise")
---

VERY few people are paying attention to this...

**And it could endanger President Trump 's entire legislative agenda!**

By all accounts, Elise Stefanik's confirmation to Trump's Cabinet will sail through. She's an excellent pick - no surprise there. That tees up a Special Election in New York to replace her. Then, the Governor is mandated by state law to lay out the election calendar.

Here's where things get ugly.

**New York Democrats are floating a scheme to DELAY the Special Election. Why would they do that, you ask? Well... it might be because it's a Conservative-leaning district, and Republicans have a _razor-thin_ margin in the House. **

_Losing one seat - or even postponing a Republican victory - will have serious ramifications for President Trump's agenda to pass through Congress. _

Democrats are so desperate to stop President Trump that they're willing to rewrite state law AND deny representation in Congress to hundreds of thousands of Americans.

What a disgrace.

_**[I won't forget this. Neither should you. That's why I'm launching an emergency fundraising blitz to fight back and save our House Majority.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=9e2d4c8548264bd8bc10f23060d5ac6d&_e=Uhx8Y3mSo_q1xFSEZUJ_8hFHOgjIp9V4k6VGXqa3EshkhjVgab4IkKhVaLkDphwe1KnmUJP_yXTAk3dXwlnQX1qwwfhtRFqTXypH1AjeGxlR7taY2-_OmOeMIHRJBQOWHcmn95qY4SBiCRSczXve7RDW71l3d0uq6PitAaw1FjFitz9-AWwubGYt7VHtfE9_JzM64OLYgNH2dwWJkcl3Qst5M9kl590w_OoTcSj5D9PDkHo82RNlfF4wZxfQc1iabCuvIPXhkz_k9KQcKIMfp24P7YIxj0uErRx8Urx0Xs8fQk07615qlF33xSvMhXr7CYXCiivTY3JNccS1YKVqbYRN1flEY1bCr49EfTI4imKocjpWOSJKxSQXJrhJ2RM-7AtfEYLq_MnilJk7MUQWeA%3D%3D "I won’t forget this. Neither should you. That’s why I’m launching an emergency fundraising blitz to fight back and save our House Majority.")**_ I set a $100,000 goal and I'd really appreciate it if you'd be willing to pitch in a few bucks today.
| | [CONTRIBUTE NOW](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=9e2d4c8548264bd8bc10f23060d5ac6d&_e=Uhx8Y3mSo_q1xFSEZUJ_8hFHOgjIp9V4k6VGXqa3EshkhjVgab4IkKhVaLkDphwe1KnmUJP_yXTAk3dXwlnQX1qwwfhtRFqTXypH1AjeGxlR7taY2-_OmOeMIHRJBQOWHcmn95qY4SBiCRSczXve7RDW71l3d0uq6PitAaw1FjFitz9-AWwubGYt7VHtfE9_JzM64OLYgNH2dwWJkcl3Qst5M9kl590w_OoTcSj5D9MNjio2WKKvYHMHhY7TR1ZByojIJS2l6AwcmDUKMs0w4Joxg2JSzELcbwN5LqE21NlX8ivr5PmXsm8Af9I02kQ9vTWd_SD8FKb_x1l2UfK0go-v4UX5KtsWaheeA3-U9C5JZZfbN2pfvmwe4LMXytdXnDZa331J1qpm40o6JEgsvw%3D%3D "CONTRIBUTE NOW")
---

Let me be very clear:

Passing President Trump's agenda is at the very top of my priority list. We need to get this right and time is of the essence.

Every seat matters a LOT when the margins are so close.

I won't stand around while a bunch of New York Democrats are trying to hamstring President Trump's entire legislative agenda.

_**[Can you please help us? I really need your support right now. It doesn't get more important than this.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=9e2d4c8548264bd8bc10f23060d5ac6d&_e=Uhx8Y3mSo_q1xFSEZUJ_8hFHOgjIp9V4k6VGXqa3EshkhjVgab4IkKhVaLkDphwe1KnmUJP_yXTAk3dXwlnQX1qwwfhtRFqTXypH1AjeGxlR7taY2-_OmOeMIHRJBQOWHcmn95qY4SBiCRSczXve7RDW71l3d0uq6PitAaw1FjFitz9-AWwubGYt7VHtfE9_JzM64OLYgNH2dwWJkcl3Qst5M9kl590w_OoTcSj5D9NQu-4fA7Kw0gqnD7Jlfygng2oofnza_DD0FS9BfCdBp3fs9olDh_7Q80xVGqYmC6NNOQ8gwZriEGBGsaMClfn0O8DR0hwqz7n77kO5oT0zEPbAuC6r10ZGdnfILXAqd4rUsU02gOYHIJ0gyAoa6RaLA-bIVUlxHtWTTqVWGarGbQ%3D%3D "Can you please help us? I really need your support right now. It doesn’t get more important than this. ")**_

Thanks for all you do,
Leader Scalise

I'm humbled to announce that I am your new House Majority Leader. My first order of business is checking in with our best supporters, so I just launched my new** MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY** for that specific reason.

name: Rep. Brandon Gill
subject: Petition: Deport Ilhan Omar
body:
I need YOUR name on this.

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Friend, we should have never let Ilhan Omar into our country.

And frankly, America would be a much better place if she were to be sent back to Somalia.
[SIGN THE PETITION - DEPORT ILHAN OMAR](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Recently, it came out that Ilhan _**Omar is hosting free workshops**_ for Somalians, who are in our country illegally, _**on how to evade ICE and deportation.**_
It could not be more blatant: _**Ilhan Omar is more loyal to illegal Somalians than she is to the United States, or the office she was elected to**_.__
_****_
_**It is an absolute disgrace.**_
[SIGN THE PETITION - DEPORT ILHAN OMAR](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
A sitting Member of Congress is facilitating a full-scale invasion of our country...
A normal society would refer to that as _**treason**_...
But regardless of labels, one thing is clear:
_**_[The time has come to arrest and deport Ilhan Omar](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**_
[SIGN THE PETITION - DEPORT ILHAN OMAR](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
| | I have had my fill of _**foreigners storming our border**_ , complaining about and disparaging our country, and then helping more and more of them invade our lands... And I know the American people are too.
__**[If you’re as sick and tired of it as I am, sign my petition and let’s send her back to Somalia where she belongs](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**__ _**.**_
---
__**[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**__Yours Sincerely,
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9Y8b/?__vbtrk=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjc0NjA3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Bottom line
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
The bottom line is that opponents of voting rights are on track to easily enact many of the proposed policies from Project 2025 . We're focused on the changes to voting and our elections, but there's no doubt that this plan is radically altering our country and our lives into a mix of our inequitable past and an unrecognizable autocracy. We cannot let the loss of our rights become irreversible.
We cannot stand by and let Project 2025 become reality. Chip in $25 or more right now to make sure every American knows about the dangerous consequences of this plan and holds Congress accountable for standing by to let this happen >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
Voter Action Project Logo [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
Courier: Trump's executive orders seem to be ripped right from the pages of Project 2025 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
Opponents of voting rights spent 2024 disavowing the Heritage Foundation's "Project 2025", Team. Now that they're in control they seem to be following that playbook line by line.
Organized by the Heritage Foundation, a right-wing, dark money, inside-the-beltway, anti-voting think tank , and advised by more than 100 conservative groups, Project 2025 put forth a 922-page list of policy recommendations, going agency by agency in the federal government.
So far the Trump administration has followed Project 2025 in five major ways:
➜ Filling the administration with the authors of Project 2025. From installing Russell Vought, the main author, as head of OMB to making the head of the FCC section head of the FCC to and more, the author's list is basically being appointed to powerful positions en masse.
➜ Mass firings in the civil service. Elon Musk's DOGE team started with a buyout scheme for federal employees and is now threatening layoffs. Project 2025 calls for replacing them with MAGA henchmen.
➜ Military immigration enforcement. They've promised to deploy U.S. troops on our domestic borders.
➜ Persecuting transgender Americans. The Trump administration has attacked gender identity across the board.
➜ Environmental rollbacks. From pulling out of the Paris Climate Accords (again) to opening Alaska's land to drilling to valuing fossil fuels over all other forms of energy production, they're dismantling environmental regulation piece by piece.
This is just the start. If they've already done all this, you can bet Project 2025's recommendations for voter suppression are coming soon . We must use this playbook of theirs against them and fight back however we can.
Please, step up and fuel our efforts to defend our fundamental right to vote and more with $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=021025_1&amount=15]
GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=021025_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=021025_1&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=021025_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=021025_1&amount=35]
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=021025_1&amount=25 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]
Donate Now [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=021025_1&amount=25]

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas poised for slower job growth this year, Dallas Fed projects,** " Texas Tribune's **Joshua Fechter** -- "Texas will likely add jobs at a slower pace in 2024 after years of robust employment growth, the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas said Friday.

The state will add nearly 284,000 jobs this year, a 2% increase from 2023, the Dallas Fed projects -- a pace that's in line with the state's job growth rate before COVID-19 hit and the state's economy kicked into overdrive. By year's end, Texas employment will hit 14.4 million jobs.

By contrast, Texas added about 427,000 jobs in 2022 at a pace of 3.1% -- the result of a rapidly growing labor force fueled by strong domestic and international migration.

"We're cleared for landing, landing back to our long run trend rate of growth," **Pia Orrenius** , Dallas Fed vice president and senior economist, said Friday.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/13d97ce5-d595-40cf-8b0f-f11bff69c22e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Still, Texas outpaced the nation as a whole in job growth last year and ranked fifth among U.S. states in the number of jobs added, behind Wyoming, Idaho, South Dakota and Nevada. Each of the state's 25 metropolitan areas saw economic growth. Tech, hit with widespread layoffs last year, was the only sector that saw job losses in the state.

"Texas' 2023 job growth exceeded expectations, pointing to the resiliency of the labor market," said **Luis Torres** , Dallas Fed senior business economist.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/13d97ce5-d595-40cf-8b0f-f11bff69c22e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
There are other bright spots in the state's economy, according to the Dallas Fed. Texas oil and gas production surpassed pre-pandemic peaks in 2023, Orrenius said, and the sector continues to add jobs.

Despite high interest rates, single-family home construction remained above pre-pandemic levels in 2023 -- though building permits for single-family homes fell by more than 5%. Homebuilders are building smaller homes in order to keep costs down and ultimately provide less expensive buying options to homebuyers, Orrenius said.

Growth in apartment rents slowed in 2023 after years of significant increases in asking rents in the state's major metros, Orrenius said -- the result of an apartment building boom in the pandemic era. Rents fell last year in places like Austin and the Dallas-Fort Worth region that previously saw a surge in rents.

Some headwinds could complicate the state's job growth this year. After months of decline, inflation began to creep up in Texas toward the end of 2023, diverging with the rest of the country. Growth in the state's sales tax revenue slowed, indicating that consumers are spending less. Fears of a potential recession among business owners have waned, but many worry that geopolitical and domestic uncertainty surrounding the 2024 presidential election could dampen their business and harm the state's economy, Orrenius said.

The Texas office market could also be a trouble spot for the state's overall economy. Despite having some of the highest return-to-office rates in the country, Texas' largest metros are [grappling with high office vacancies](https://substack.com/redirect/ba4f2c72-16f3-4205-b9f5-9fb2d7f20860?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- creating uncertainty in the commercial real estate market." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/c17114ff-a13c-4062-8226-ecf9908da3fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS "Texas poised for slower job growth this year, Dallas Fed projects, " Texas Tribune's Joshua Fechter -- "Texas will likely add jobs at a slower pace in 2024 after years of robust employment growth, the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas said Friday. The state will add nearly 284,000 jobs this year, a 2% increase from 2023, the Dallas Fed projects -- a pace that's in line with the state's job growth rate before COVID-19 hit and the state's economy kicked into overdrive. By year's end, Texas employment will hit 14.4 million jobs. By contrast, Texas added about 427,000 jobs in 2022 at a pace of 3.1% -- the result of a rapidly growing labor force fueled by strong domestic and international migration. "We're cleared for landing, landing back to our long run trend rate of growth," Pia Orrenius , Dallas Fed vice president and senior economist, said Friday. Still, Texas outpaced the nation as a whole in job growth last year and ranked fifth among U.S. states in the number of jobs added, behind Wyoming, Idaho, South Dakota and Nevada. Each of the state's 25 metropolitan areas saw economic growth. Tech, hit with widespread layoffs last year, was the only sector that saw job losses in the state. "Texas' 2023 job growth exceeded expectations, pointing to the resiliency of the labor market," said Luis Torres , Dallas Fed senior business economist. There are other bright spots in the state's economy, according to the Dallas Fed. Texas oil and gas production surpassed pre-pandemic peaks in 2023, Orrenius said, and the sector continues to add jobs. Despite high interest rates, single-family home construction remained above pre-pandemic levels in 2023 -- though building permits for single-family homes fell by more than 5%. Homebuilders are building smaller homes in order to keep costs down and ultimately provide less expensive buying options to homebuyers, Orrenius said. Growth in apartment rents slowed in 2023 after years of significant increases in asking rents in the state's major metros, Orrenius said -- the result of an apartment building boom in the pandemic era. Rents fell last year in places like Austin and the Dallas-Fort Worth region that previously saw a surge in rents. Some headwinds could complicate the state's job growth this year. After months of decline, inflation began to creep up in Texas toward the end of 2023, diverging with the rest of the country. Growth in the state's sales tax revenue slowed, indicating that consumers are spending less. Fears of a potential recession among business owners have waned, but many worry that geopolitical and domestic uncertainty surrounding the 2024 presidential election could dampen their business and harm the state's economy, Orrenius said. The Texas office market could also be a trouble spot for the state's overall economy. Despite having some of the highest return-to-office rates in the country, Texas' largest metros are -- creating uncertainty in the commercial real estate market." FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas poised for slower job growth this year, Dallas Fed projects,** " Texas Tribune's **Joshua Fechter** -- "Texas will likely add jobs at a slower pace in 2024 after years of robust employment growth, the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas said Friday.

The state will add nearly 284,000 jobs this year, a 2% increase from 2023, the Dallas Fed projects -- a pace that's in line with the state's job growth rate before COVID-19 hit and the state's economy kicked into overdrive. By year's end, Texas employment will hit 14.4 million jobs.

By contrast, Texas added about 427,000 jobs in 2022 at a pace of 3.1% -- the result of a rapidly growing labor force fueled by strong domestic and international migration.

"We're cleared for landing, landing back to our long run trend rate of growth," **Pia Orrenius** , Dallas Fed vice president and senior economist, said Friday.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/13986c71-2516-4c4c-814a-7be0a7d39b0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Still, Texas outpaced the nation as a whole in job growth last year and ranked fifth among U.S. states in the number of jobs added, behind Wyoming, Idaho, South Dakota and Nevada. Each of the state's 25 metropolitan areas saw economic growth. Tech, hit with widespread layoffs last year, was the only sector that saw job losses in the state.

"Texas' 2023 job growth exceeded expectations, pointing to the resiliency of the labor market," said **Luis Torres** , Dallas Fed senior business economist.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/13986c71-2516-4c4c-814a-7be0a7d39b0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
There are other bright spots in the state's economy, according to the Dallas Fed. Texas oil and gas production surpassed pre-pandemic peaks in 2023, Orrenius said, and the sector continues to add jobs.

Despite high interest rates, single-family home construction remained above pre-pandemic levels in 2023 -- though building permits for single-family homes fell by more than 5%. Homebuilders are building smaller homes in order to keep costs down and ultimately provide less expensive buying options to homebuyers, Orrenius said.

Growth in apartment rents slowed in 2023 after years of significant increases in asking rents in the state's major metros, Orrenius said -- the result of an apartment building boom in the pandemic era. Rents fell last year in places like Austin and the Dallas-Fort Worth region that previously saw a surge in rents.

Some headwinds could complicate the state's job growth this year. After months of decline, inflation began to creep up in Texas toward the end of 2023, diverging with the rest of the country. Growth in the state's sales tax revenue slowed, indicating that consumers are spending less. Fears of a potential recession among business owners have waned, but many worry that geopolitical and domestic uncertainty surrounding the 2024 presidential election could dampen their business and harm the state's economy, Orrenius said.

The Texas office market could also be a trouble spot for the state's overall economy. Despite having some of the highest return-to-office rates in the country, Texas' largest metros are [grappling with high office vacancies](https://substack.com/redirect/3f6bac3e-9115-4696-a8d0-8c7a10c0501d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- creating uncertainty in the commercial real estate market." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/c2883536-214f-4b65-9fad-a3c9cec08f23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS "Texas poised for slower job growth this year, Dallas Fed projects, " Texas Tribune's Joshua Fechter -- "Texas will likely add jobs at a slower pace in 2024 after years of robust employment growth, the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas said Friday. The state will add nearly 284,000 jobs this year, a 2% increase from 2023, the Dallas Fed projects -- a pace that's in line with the state's job growth rate before COVID-19 hit and the state's economy kicked into overdrive. By year's end, Texas employment will hit 14.4 million jobs. By contrast, Texas added about 427,000 jobs in 2022 at a pace of 3.1% -- the result of a rapidly growing labor force fueled by strong domestic and international migration. "We're cleared for landing, landing back to our long run trend rate of growth," Pia Orrenius , Dallas Fed vice president and senior economist, said Friday. Still, Texas outpaced the nation as a whole in job growth last year and ranked fifth among U.S. states in the number of jobs added, behind Wyoming, Idaho, South Dakota and Nevada. Each of the state's 25 metropolitan areas saw economic growth. Tech, hit with widespread layoffs last year, was the only sector that saw job losses in the state. "Texas' 2023 job growth exceeded expectations, pointing to the resiliency of the labor market," said Luis Torres , Dallas Fed senior business economist. There are other bright spots in the state's economy, according to the Dallas Fed. Texas oil and gas production surpassed pre-pandemic peaks in 2023, Orrenius said, and the sector continues to add jobs. Despite high interest rates, single-family home construction remained above pre-pandemic levels in 2023 -- though building permits for single-family homes fell by more than 5%. Homebuilders are building smaller homes in order to keep costs down and ultimately provide less expensive buying options to homebuyers, Orrenius said. Growth in apartment rents slowed in 2023 after years of significant increases in asking rents in the state's major metros, Orrenius said -- the result of an apartment building boom in the pandemic era. Rents fell last year in places like Austin and the Dallas-Fort Worth region that previously saw a surge in rents. Some headwinds could complicate the state's job growth this year. After months of decline, inflation began to creep up in Texas toward the end of 2023, diverging with the rest of the country. Growth in the state's sales tax revenue slowed, indicating that consumers are spending less. Fears of a potential recession among business owners have waned, but many worry that geopolitical and domestic uncertainty surrounding the 2024 presidential election could dampen their business and harm the state's economy, Orrenius said. The Texas office market could also be a trouble spot for the state's overall economy. Despite having some of the highest return-to-office rates in the country, Texas' largest metros are -- creating uncertainty in the commercial real estate market." * **TOP NEWS**

“**Texas poised for slower job growth this year, Dallas Fed projects,** ” Texas Tribune's **Joshua Fechter** — “Texas will likely add jobs at a slower pace in 2024 after years of robust employment growth, the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas said Friday.

The state will add nearly 284,000 jobs this year, a 2% increase from 2023, the Dallas Fed projects — a pace that’s in line with the state’s job growth rate before COVID-19 hit and the state’s economy kicked into overdrive. By year’s end, Texas employment will hit 14.4 million jobs.

By contrast, Texas added about 427,000 jobs in 2022 at a pace of 3.1% — the result of a rapidly growing labor force fueled by strong domestic and international migration.

“We're cleared for landing, landing back to our long run trend rate of growth,” **Pia Orrenius** , Dallas Fed vice president and senior economist, said Friday.

Still, Texas outpaced the nation as a whole in job growth last year and ranked fifth among U.S. states in the number of jobs added, behind Wyoming, Idaho, South Dakota and Nevada. Each of the state’s 25 metropolitan areas saw economic growth. Tech, hit with widespread layoffs last year, was the only sector that saw job losses in the state.

“Texas’ 2023 job growth exceeded expectations, pointing to the resiliency of the labor market,” said **Luis Torres** , Dallas Fed senior business economist.

There are other bright spots in the state’s economy, according to the Dallas Fed. Texas oil and gas production surpassed pre-pandemic peaks in 2023, Orrenius said, and the sector continues to add jobs.

Despite high interest rates, single-family home construction remained above pre-pandemic levels in 2023 — though building permits for single-family homes fell by more than 5%. Homebuilders are building smaller homes in order to keep costs down and ultimately provide less expensive buying options to homebuyers, Orrenius said.

Growth in apartment rents slowed in 2023 after years of significant increases in asking rents in the state’s major metros, Orrenius said — the result of an apartment building boom in the pandemic era. Rents fell last year in places like Austin and the Dallas-Fort Worth region that previously saw a surge in rents.

Some headwinds could complicate the state’s job growth this year. After months of decline, inflation began to creep up in Texas toward the end of 2023, diverging with the rest of the country. Growth in the state’s sales tax revenue slowed, indicating that consumers are spending less. Fears of a potential recession among business owners have waned, but many worry that geopolitical and domestic uncertainty surrounding the 2024 presidential election could dampen their business and harm the state’s economy, Orrenius said.

The Texas office market could also be a trouble spot for the state’s overall economy. Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | TOP NEWS “Texas poised for slower job growth this year, Dallas Fed projects, ” Texas Tribune's Joshua Fechter — “Texas will likely add jobs at a slower pace in 2024 after years of robust employment growth, the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas said Friday. The state will add nearly 284,000 jobs this year, a 2% increase from 2023, the Dallas Fed projects — a pace that’s in line with the state’s job growth rate before COVID-19 hit and the state’s economy kicked into overdrive. By year’s end, Texas employment will hit 14.4 million jobs. By contrast, Texas added about 427,000 jobs in 2022 at a pace of 3.1% — the result of a rapidly growing labor force fueled by strong domestic and international migration. “We're cleared for landing, landing back to our long run trend rate of growth,” Pia Orrenius , Dallas Fed vice president and senior economist, said Friday. » Still, Texas outpaced the nation as a whole in job growth last year and ranked fifth among U.S. states in the number of jobs added, behind Wyoming, Idaho, South Dakota and Nevada. Each of the state’s 25 metropolitan areas saw economic growth. Tech, hit with widespread layoffs last year, was the only sector that saw job losses in the state. “Texas’ 2023 job growth exceeded expectations, pointing to the resiliency of the labor market,” said Luis Torres , Dallas Fed senior business economist. » There are other bright spots in the state’s economy, according to the Dallas Fed. Texas oil and gas production surpassed pre-pandemic peaks in 2023, Orrenius said, and the sector continues to add jobs. Despite high interest rates, single-family home construction remained above pre-pandemic levels in 2023 — though building permits for single-family homes fell by more than 5%. Homebuilders are building smaller homes in order to keep costs down and ultimately provide less expensive buying options to homebuyers, Orrenius said. Growth in apartment rents slowed in 2023 after years of significant increases in asking rents in the state’s major metros, Orrenius said — the result of an apartment building boom in the pandemic era. Rents fell last year in places like Austin and the Dallas-Fort Worth region that previously saw a surge in rents. Some headwinds could complicate the state’s job growth this year. After months of decline, inflation began to creep up in Texas toward the end of 2023, diverging with the rest of the country. Growth in the state’s sales tax revenue slowed, indicating that consumers are spending less. Fears of a potential recession among business owners have waned, but many worry that geopolitical and domestic uncertainty surrounding the 2024 presidential election could dampen their business and harm the state’s economy, Orrenius said. The Texas office market could also be a trouble spot for the state’s overall economy. Despite having some of the highest return-to-office rates in the country, Texas’ largest metros are — creating uncertainty in the commercial real estate market." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Keith Ellison
subject: A huge victory
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417
body:
Peter,

The Trump administration broke the law when they gave Elon Musk and his so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) access to the Treasury Department’s central payment system, which contains Americans’ bank account details, Social Security numbers, and other highly sensitive data.

To protect your right to privacy, I joined 18 other attorneys general in filing a lawsuit to block Musk’s access to personal information. In response, a federal judge barred DOGE from accessing private data and ordered the unauthorized individuals to destroy any copies of the data they had made.

This is a victory for Minnesotans, but the work to protect our democracy continues.

Our records indicate that you’re still eligible for Minnesota’s Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program. That means you can donate up to $75 (or $150 for couples) to our campaign and receive a full refund from the State of Minnesota.

Peter, will you make a fully refundable $75 donation today to help power this grassroots movement and keep up the fight?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
CHIP IN $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
CHIP IN $25 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
CHIP IN $75 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?express_lane=true&amount=75&refcode=express_lane_75]
CHIP IN $150 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?express_lane=true&amount=150&refcode=express_lane_150]
CHIP IN $250 NOW [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?express_lane=true&amount=250&refcode=express_lane_250]
OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?refcode=other]

Thank you,

Keith Ellison

Minnesota's Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program reset on January 1. You can now use your 2025 PCR to contribute up to $75 (or $150 for couples) and receive a full refund from the State of Minnesota.

Please take advantage of your PCR and make a REFUNDABLE $75 DONATION so Keith can continue fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect.

CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250210ke?refcode=body&amount=25]

Prepared and paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417

Minneapolis, MN 55417
United States

name: U.S. Senator Tim Sheehy
subject: This is Navy SEAL Tim Sheehy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
I'm coming to you for backup

Friend, it's Navy SEAL and U.S. Senator Tim Sheehy.

I'm new to the Senate, but I'm no stranger to a tough fight.

When it comes to confirming President Trump's team, a tough fight is what we're getting from Democrats. They're doing everything they can to obstruct the process.

But Senate Republicans were elected to win tough fights.

_If that means working nights, weekends, and holidays, so be it._

**[Whatever it takes to get President Trump's Cabinet in place so we can get America back on track.](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=dfa3634cdb694c01a99974ae640bbeb6&_e=AKYuEhZuXhQLWtVTp0hIr56l0d7Z0T2h2IkPtcnKPZQIuhLDtcdMnuLUned20eSEnydjfOk2QpyImghyJiOXqOTpjNiZoqygItMP2J0zPfNBGLRE-WXSDGvOESS3uoOgA2r_nl3rVycNFFmQ3nYu9K3_jwvPzyNwrnvJIILNNSwOxPGS0UhYNerXGl2pQI3ZoJn1PA6Y-mSlemsO3L_04WrHwNC0WatojmOs6V1Ko9KVEUjoHtugbomEYpIYBelUalAJum_LNYkuKZ6wceCNx9qvl18o5ckFm0fifu8m7plo_gvPZzYqvJVL6fZxv-DqxcOEm7irmhKI0xxCsCpNW1bOzEDYFsKqaOPBNsgkVhg0wQYVYvTQdwBIAt6eFMKpAjD3S89idj3feeISVNU-q1jSJEm41ENOPKe-O0z0jH1QeTRKsI-E-hXlmq05cf3HpoWS_luGv7ACNfrCLLZLxn-gJi_INsrEIClj9sLM0Gg%3D)**

name: THE ECONOMY
subject: Billionaires Like Elon Musk Shouldn’t Control ME
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gay Valimont Gay Valimont For Congress
body:
| | |
---
Friend, **Elon Musk wants to reshape our economy to benefit himself—again.** His latest scheme? A Treasury payment system built around Dogecoin. Yes, you read that right. While working families struggle with rising costs, Trump’s allies are busy pushing policies that benefit billionaires and their pet projects. This is Gay Valimont, and **without your help before special elections**
---
---
**We’ve seen this before. Corporate elites like Musk bankroll politicians who will protect their wealth, then use that influence to manipulate our economy.** Meanwhile, everyday Americans get left behind. Instead of investing in working families, affordable healthcare, and strong public schools, Trump’s circle is focused on lining their own pockets.

name: Fight Trump via Our Revolution
subject: Trump is weaponizing federal law enforcement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21467?t=1011?refcode=email-20250210_v&akid=7687%2E5885482%2EhK0kaH ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis — Trump is
WEAPONIZING federal law enforcement to target political enemies and
PURGING officials at the DOJ and FBI. This is exactly what dictators do —
and we must fight back.

Trump 2.0 is remaking federal law enforcement into a political weapon for
his personal use. Trump’s nominee for FBI Director, Kash Patel, has his
own enemies list, too — forcing career officials into hiding out of fear
for their lives.

Trump-appointed U.S. Attorney for D.C. said he would take legal action
against anyone who impedes or threatens Elon Musk’s DOGE team. They’re
making it a crime to oppose this administration. And what happens when a
judge rules against DOGE? Musk ATTACKS and threatens their job.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21468?t=1061&akid=7687%2E5885482%2EhK0kaH ][IMG]

Our Citizen Action Team of 30,000 activists (and counting) are organizing
in every congressional district in the country and pressuring Senators to
reject Patel and Trump’s other dangerous nominees. Our Revolution is
stepping up to fight the authoritarian shift under Trump, hold the line on
Trump’s attacks, and shed light on the dangers of Democrats sitting this
out on the sidelines.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21467?t=1057?express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20250210nd&akid=7687%2E5885482%2EhK0kaH ]Will you rush a donation to support our urgent work to stop the
frightening Trump-Musk weaponization of our government? We need the
support of our grassroots donors at this unprecedented moment in our
history.

Influence Task Force, an important unit to protect America from insidious
foreign corruption in our politics and elections.

Trump lied to us. Not only is he taking words directly from Project 2025, but he staffed his administration with right-wing officials who wrote the agenda. The Senate is the first line of defense against Trump and his far-right agenda, but Senate Republicans refuse to hold him accountable. [HB1572 \- Cigars; TAX to collect data on number sold with sales price equal or greater than $1.50, report.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yOASxSQyTbwSMloUPP_FWYRnf3jvOZACb-I2_ZNqp_4Oh8GnO2V9xaC0nUgVbTvhP0p0nxH3O84iscVBGFFSaJUEtOXMm5p2PHzk6YNSH61lFqudl-msdu98rUIeRj2OG4ulOq6NQ0C-&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==) Patron: Ware Department of Taxation; tobacco products tax; cigars; report. Directs the Department of Taxation to update its form TT-8, Virginia Tobacco Products Tax Return (OTP), to begin collecting data on the number of cigars sold in the Commonwealth at a sales price equal to or greater than $1.50 as a distinct category from all other cigars. The bill directs the Department to update such form or devise another appropriate method for data collection as soon as practicable and provide a report of the data so collected and estimated fiscal impacts of adopting a cap of 30 cents ($0.30) per cigar or another rate on the tobacco products tax imposed on this category of cigars to the Chairmen of the House Committee on Finance and the Senate Committee on Finance and Appropriations no later than January 15, 2026.

1\. The exemption for discharged loans for eligible veterans currently set to expire January 1, 2026;
2\. The credit for amounts paid to another state for income taxes of a pass-through entity currently set to expire January 1, 2026;
3\. The standard deduction in an amount equal to $8,500 for single individuals and $17,000 for married persons currently set to revert to $3,000 for single individuals and $6,000 for married persons as of January 1, 2026;
4\. The deduction for eligible educator expenses currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
5\. The tax credit for low-income taxpayers currently set to expire January 1, 2026;
6\. The tax credit for reforestation and afforestation currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
7\. The election for eligible owners of pass-through entities to be taxed at the entity level currently set to expire January 1, 2026;
8\. The subtractions from the numerators of the property and payroll apportionment factors for eligible companies for (i) property acquired in any qualified locality before January 1, 2025, and (ii) payroll attributable to jobs created within such locality before January 1, 2025;
9\. The major business facility job tax credit currently set to expire July 1, 2025;
10\. The worker training tax credit for (i) expenses incurred by a business for eligible worker training currently set to expire July 1, 2025, and (ii) direct costs incurred by a business engaged primarily in manufacturing in conducting orientation, instruction, and training in the Commonwealth, relating to the manufacturing activities undertaken by the business currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
11\. The tax credit for purchase of machinery and equipment used for advanced recycling and processing recyclable materials currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
12\. The tax credit for participating landlords renting qualified housing units (i) in eligible census tracts currently set to expire January 1, 2026, and (ii) in eligible non-metropolitan census tracts currently set to expire January 1, 2026;
13\. The tax credit for green and alternative energy job creation currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
14\. The tax credit for qualified research and development expenses currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
15\. The tax credit for Virginia qualified major research and development expenses currently set to expire January 1, 2025;
16\. The exemption from sales and use taxation for gold, silver, or platinum bullion or legal tender coins currently set to expire June 30, 2025;
17\. The exemption from sales and use taxation for certain printed materials purchased from an advertising business from a printer within the Commonwealth and distributed outside the Commonwealth before July 1, 2025; and
18\. The exemptions from sales and use taxation for (i) parts, engines, and supplies for aviation component parts currently set to expire July 1, 2025, and (ii) prescription medicines and drugs purchased by veterinarians currently set to expire July 1, 2025.
[HB2397 \- Virginia Growth and Opportunity Act; expands eligibility for grants.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yOASxSQyTbwSkUfvRrlEbd3x3xoK9OkmYEDQjHlsEEBRX_SxTtyURZEq2PKKwHR3H1-Iur3E2zHQQO67jwJExNjcBfZl4eSh9ZPPivFIPExg-2UjHkxfaYNBSk2TAlMCP60hNg1J_cHm&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==) Patron: Glass Virginia Growth and Opportunity Act; eligibility for grants. Expands eligibility for regional councils to receive Virginia Growth and Opportunity Grants. The bill would allow regional councils to apply for and utilize grant funds for regional activities that substantially promote the objectives of the council's economic growth and diversification plan where there exists a demonstrable gap in services that negatively impacts a region's ability to grow one or more trade sector industry clusters.
[HB2485 \- Cannabis control; establishes framework for creating retail market, penalties.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yDwf5ig8n2LDGArSO8v5vm48v5BUokFlB7hlZXzT6Mops52-t4WS1GI72IgLi1YVNDwpx41MrsAOaDTVM8PFQy5MLbW8bjNxUf7R--x5Lhlac2OwB-MPaRy2cmOqvKKAEOZUDPxe6odn&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==) Patron: Krizek Cannabis control; retail market; penalties. Establishes a framework for the creation of a retail marijuana market in the Commonwealth, to be administered by the Virginia Cannabis Control Authority. The bill allows the Authority to begin issuing all marijuana licenses on September 1, 2025, but provides that no retail sales may occur prior to May 1, 2026.

### [HB1894 State correctional facilities; cell and facility conditions, temperature monitoring.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yOASxSQyTbwSdnNIu2GQ2pJbBKydYLTkoZsaNXftdDBsn8kbEAFoE3pauTqLfAWiRGLDMklIKS7YkrvFyZc7DPTE-qVUjG2jgpLjGy6RtnFHILu-FgrXGvbT8Ho7ilmuRL0lBhqBdvPz&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==)
Introduced by: [Holly M. Seibold (Chief Patron)](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yOASxSQyTbwSazCCULtgnYzrWZ-mz8hCRzzQMJT-MKqWrfBYTj4XNKkH7YWqnyCV6ET8vKgTCm_fekEIBGEYlgHWqzbKfQEKOO8grOsuS2AvBuAVWC28wGIDuiQVL_TDs-sShTMn44rK5-tG3AEMzItRJkY6rVCVdOUQUmDltDZqwZiF25s7JVcZBpzkbNbBSw==&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==) State correctional facilities; cell conditions; temperature monitoring; report. Requires the Department of Corrections to require that heat and air conditioning be provided in all state correctional facilities that are capable of controlling the interior temperature of such facility so that a temperature of not less than 65 degrees Fahrenheit or more than 80 degrees Fahrenheit is maintained in each facility. The bill also directs the Department to evaluate the implementation of remote temperature monitoring and to report its findings to the General Assembly by October 1, 2025.

[HB1844 \- Baby Food Protection Act; testing and labeling requirements for toxic heavy metals.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yOASxSQyTbwSeHBrUBMUblXvM94Z6D3pdrNBteVzNRHNf-MYL5WoX67sDBuIQEpAoIxSPDw-CekJSV-s74dA_RvNxFb8iJCRNKWnE-Uf379BmEqsamEsnDrY6NunUchE2Z5ye7tyAOXh&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==) Patron: Maldonado Baby Food Protection Act; testing and labeling requirements for toxic heavy metals. Prohibits a person from selling, distributing, or offering for sale a baby food product that contains certain toxic heavy metals that exceed the limits established by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The bill requires a manufacturer of a baby food product to meet certain testing requirements and include certain information related to toxic heavy metals on the manufacturer's website and on the baby food product. The bill requires a consumer to report a baby food product to the Commissioner of Agriculture and Consumer Services if the consumer reasonably believes that the baby food is being sold in the Commonwealth with toxic heavy metals that exceed the limits established by the FDA. The foregoing provisions of the bill have a delayed effective date of January 1, 2026. The bill also directs the Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services to convene a work group to study and make recommendations on the current enforcement of laws related to the presence of toxic heavy metals in baby food products and any further action needed to address the issue of toxic heavy metals in baby food products.

| [HB1716 \- Contraception; establishes right to obtain, applicability, enforcement.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017RtszQEflynxsbcjganyY8xQT6rLaTYBmwsokGvJh86_ivcWnxo1yOASxSQyTbwS63QqA0MQXt4at7pSaZDr6cwhXPMwNYywuEFJW4l62V7w1xnMOYS-lqg5MwJmr7w_9o2xLD2w2J9V9nmsU4EhCMeoUAmhbRka89BvcsS2xL6lz42ZRRrIhvmzboQQfOcp&c=9GZWCsEYhbmD1q5Vm9tY3Nxnknx5MHAr3Iox6PTuFB48Fr6l1M19Qg==&ch=Hw64hgauvjebEPW6j_edoc5nf6z3OsymeQT1aghM3j2c89bvkdRZJg==) Patron: Price Contraception; right to contraception; applicability; enforcement. Establishes a right to obtain contraceptives and engage in contraception, as defined in the bill. The bill clarifies that none of its provisions shall be construed to permit or sanction the performance of any sterilization procedure without a patient's voluntary and informed consent. The bill creates a cause of action that may be instituted against anyone who infringes on such right.

| Dear Hello Next Saturday is the last day of session. The Senate is finishing up House bills, which seem mostly to be "companion" bills to the Senate bills we have already seen the Senate prior to crossover.
I've identified a few House bills of interest that are still alive in the Senate and coming up tomorrow. As always, for a full listing just click on the name of the committee.

clean_body: The 2025 General Assembly Session | | | | 2025 THE HONEY BADGER REPORT | | | | | | | | | 2025 SESSION Tuesday What can you expect today? | 10 DAYS REMAINING | ATTEND IN PERSON OR CALL IN TO THE MEETING LIVE | Tuesday Senate Committee Meetings | == == == == == == == == == == == == == == == == BILLS COMING UP TODAY BY COMMITTEE | == == Patron: Ware Department of Taxation; tobacco products tax; cigars; report. Directs the Department of Taxation to update its form TT-8, Virginia Tobacco Products Tax Return (OTP), to begin collecting data on the number of cigars sold in the Commonwealth at a sales price equal to or greater than $1.50 as a distinct category from all other cigars. The bill directs the Department to update such form or devise another appropriate method for data collection as soon as practicable and provide a report of the data so collected and estimated fiscal impacts of adopting a cap of 30 cents ($0.30) per cigar or another rate on the tobacco products tax imposed on this category of cigars to the Chairmen of the House Committee on Finance and the Senate Committee on Finance and Appropriations no later than January 15, 2026. == Patron: Torian Budget Bill. Amends items of and adds items to Chapter 2 of the Acts of Assembly of 2024, Special Session I. == Patron: Watts Taxation; extension of expiring sunsets. Extends to taxable year 2026: 1\. The exemption for discharged loans for eligible veterans currently set to expire January 1, 2026; 2\. The credit for amounts paid to another state for income taxes of a pass-through entity currently set to expire January 1, 2026; 3\. The standard deduction in an amount equal to $8,500 for single individuals and $17,000 for married persons currently set to revert to $3,000 for single individuals and $6,000 for married persons as of January 1, 2026; 4\. The deduction for eligible educator expenses currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 5\. The tax credit for low-income taxpayers currently set to expire January 1, 2026; 6\. The tax credit for reforestation and afforestation currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 7\. The election for eligible owners of pass-through entities to be taxed at the entity level currently set to expire January 1, 2026; 8\. The subtractions from the numerators of the property and payroll apportionment factors for eligible companies for (i) property acquired in any qualified locality before January 1, 2025, and (ii) payroll attributable to jobs created within such locality before January 1, 2025; 9\. The major business facility job tax credit currently set to expire July 1, 2025; 10\. The worker training tax credit for (i) expenses incurred by a business for eligible worker training currently set to expire July 1, 2025, and (ii) direct costs incurred by a business engaged primarily in manufacturing in conducting orientation, instruction, and training in the Commonwealth, relating to the manufacturing activities undertaken by the business currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 11\. The tax credit for purchase of machinery and equipment used for advanced recycling and processing recyclable materials currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 12\. The tax credit for participating landlords renting qualified housing units (i) in eligible census tracts currently set to expire January 1, 2026, and (ii) in eligible non-metropolitan census tracts currently set to expire January 1, 2026; 13\. The tax credit for green and alternative energy job creation currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 14\. The tax credit for qualified research and development expenses currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 15\. The tax credit for Virginia qualified major research and development expenses currently set to expire January 1, 2025; 16\. The exemption from sales and use taxation for gold, silver, or platinum bullion or legal tender coins currently set to expire June 30, 2025; 17\. The exemption from sales and use taxation for certain printed materials purchased from an advertising business from a printer within the Commonwealth and distributed outside the Commonwealth before July 1, 2025; and 18\. The exemptions from sales and use taxation for (i) parts, engines, and supplies for aviation component parts currently set to expire July 1, 2025, and (ii) prescription medicines and drugs purchased by veterinarians currently set to expire July 1, 2025. == Patron: Glass Virginia Growth and Opportunity Act; eligibility for grants. Expands eligibility for regional councils to receive Virginia Growth and Opportunity Grants. The bill would allow regional councils to apply for and utilize grant funds for regional activities that substantially promote the objectives of the council's economic growth and diversification plan where there exists a demonstrable gap in services that negatively impacts a region's ability to grow one or more trade sector industry clusters. == Patron: Krizek Cannabis control; retail market; penalties. Establishes a framework for the creation of a retail marijuana market in the Commonwealth, to be administered by the Virginia Cannabis Control Authority. The bill allows the Authority to begin issuing all marijuana licenses on September 1, 2025, but provides that no retail sales may occur prior to May 1, 2026. | == == Introduced by: == State correctional facilities; cell conditions; temperature monitoring; report. Requires the Department of Corrections to require that heat and air conditioning be provided in all state correctional facilities that are capable of controlling the interior temperature of such facility so that a temperature of not less than 65 degrees Fahrenheit or more than 80 degrees Fahrenheit is maintained in each facility. The bill also directs the Department to evaluate the implementation of remote temperature monitoring and to report its findings to the General Assembly by October 1, 2025. | == == Patron: Maldonado Baby Food Protection Act; testing and labeling requirements for toxic heavy metals. Prohibits a person from selling, distributing, or offering for sale a baby food product that contains certain toxic heavy metals that exceed the limits established by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The bill requires a manufacturer of a baby food product to meet certain testing requirements and include certain information related to toxic heavy metals on the manufacturer's website and on the baby food product. The bill requires a consumer to report a baby food product to the Commissioner of Agriculture and Consumer Services if the consumer reasonably believes that the baby food is being sold in the Commonwealth with toxic heavy metals that exceed the limits established by the FDA. The foregoing provisions of the bill have a delayed effective date of January 1, 2026. The bill also directs the Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services to convene a work group to study and make recommendations on the current enforcement of laws related to the presence of toxic heavy metals in baby food products and any further action needed to address the issue of toxic heavy metals in baby food products. | == | == Patron: Maldonado Baby Food Protection Act; testing and labeling requirements for toxic heavy metals. Prohibits a person from selling, distributing, or offering for sale a baby food product that contains certain toxic heavy metals that exceed the limits established by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The bill requires a manufacturer of a baby food product to meet certain testing requirements and include certain information related to toxic heavy metals on the manufacturer's website and on the baby food product. The bill requires a consumer to report a baby food product to the Commissioner of Agriculture and Consumer Services if the consumer reasonably believes that the baby food is being sold in the Commonwealth with toxic heavy metals that exceed the limits established by the FDA. The foregoing provisions of the bill have a delayed effective date of January 1, 2026. The bill also directs the Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services to convene a work group to study and make recommendations on the current enforcement of laws related to the presence of toxic heavy metals in baby food products and any further action needed to address the issue of toxic heavy metals in baby food products. | == | == Patron: Price Contraception; right to contraception; applicability; enforcement. Establishes a right to obtain contraceptives and engage in contraception, as defined in the bill. The bill clarifies that none of its provisions shall be construed to permit or sanction the performance of any sterilization procedure without a patient's voluntary and informed consent. The bill creates a cause of action that may be instituted against anyone who infringes on such right. | AMANDA CHASE | VIRGINIA'S HONEY BADGER | Dear Hello Next Saturday is the last day of session. The Senate is finishing up House bills, which seem mostly to be "companion" bills to the Senate bills we have already seen the Senate prior to crossover. I've identified a few House bills of interest that are still alive in the Senate and coming up tomorrow. As always, for a full listing just click on the name of the committee. Notice that the budget amendments that passed the House is now before the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee tomorrow. Click == to look at the budget amendments passed by the House to the original budget passed during Special Session I. This House bill seeks to amend and reenact Chapter 2 of the Acts of Assembly of 2024, Special Session I, as amended by Chapters 3 and 4 of the Acts of Assembly of 2024, Special Session I, which appropriates the public revenues for two years ending, respectively, on June 30, 2025, and June 30, 2026. In other words, any changes made to the == are in this House bill. For the full list of changes or amendments you can click == == Suggested amendments or edits to the original budget are available by legislator, if you're curious. These are changes they wanted to make to the original budget that Governor Youngkin introduced back in December. It's pretty enlightening to see where Virginia legislators have chosen to spend our taxpayer dollars. I hope you'll take a look. | | I want to thank you for your kind and thoughtful notes and emails, stating that you find the Honey Badger Report helpful in following what's going on in Richmond, while the General Assembly is in session. I also want to thank you for your generous donations which allow me to provide this free service to you. We need active and curious citizens to scour our budget for waste of our taxpayer funds. You would be amazed. We need our own version of DOGE here in Virginia. And that begins with you. | | | | | | | | | IMPORTANT DATES | 2025 LEGISLATIVE SESSION IMPORTANT DATES Begins: Wednesday, January 8 at noon Crossover: Tuesday, February 4 Ends: Saturday, February 22 Last Day for Governor's Actions: Monday, March 24 Reconvene: Wednesday, April 2 | IMPORTANT LINKS | | == | | == | == | | == | SUPPORT AMANDA HERE | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 250 | Appomattox, VA 24522 US | | | | | | |

clean_body: Who should be audited next?| | | Peter, Donald Trump said, "We have to solve the efficiency problem. We have to solve the fraud, waste, and abuse—all the things that have gone into the government." Elon Musk and his brilliant team have already unearthed some shocking revelations about how YOUR tax dollars are wasted. My question is this—who should they look into next? ** | | | | DOGE HAS MADE WAVES IN THEIR AUDIT OF USAID. WHO SHOULD DOGE AUDIT NEXT? ** | | | | Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee | |

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: Women's right to choose is under attack
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate Contributions or gifts to Shaheen for Senate are not tax deductible
body:

| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101897890/527420592/97232231?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&code=jtk3069-su-nd&refcode=jtk3069-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDQvMS84ODk1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNzhhMmM2OS1lY2U3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aVGtTvzGBhHwhkWxB-U6MdisQDFkX9bGZEe4n4dJtqo=&emci=e025589c-80e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=f78a2c69-ece7-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=858201)

Friend, since Day One, Donald Trump has made it his mission to further undermine our reproductive freedoms.
From rescinding Biden-era executive orders that expanded access to reproductive health care – including access to abortion and contraception – to pardoning hoards of anti-abortion extremists, Trump has made his position clear: **Women's right to choose is under attack.**
**These alarming moves in the White House set a dangerous precedent for the future of reproductive rights, putting us one step closer to a nationwide abortion ban.**
I, for one, am not about to stand idly by while these anti-choice policies wreak havoc on our nation. I am prepared to do everything in my power to fight for our bodily autonomy, but I need to know where you stand.

I know just how passionate you are about protecting our most fundamental freedoms, friend. **That's why I've personally selected you to represent your area in my urgent reproductive rights poll._[Please, help us reach 300 responses before submissions close at midnight and tell us:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101897892/527420594/97232231?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100&code=jtk3069-su-nd&refcode=jtk3069-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDQvMS84ODk1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNzhhMmM2OS1lY2U3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aVGtTvzGBhHwhkWxB-U6MdisQDFkX9bGZEe4n4dJtqo=&emci=e025589c-80e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=f78a2c69-ece7-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=858201)_**
| | **Do you support reproductive rights?**

clean_body: PREVIEW TEXT GOES HERE | | | Friend, since Day One, Donald Trump has made it his mission to further undermine our reproductive freedoms. From rescinding Biden-era executive orders that expanded access to reproductive health care – including access to abortion and contraception – to pardoning hoards of anti-abortion extremists, Trump has made his position clear: Women's right to choose is under attack. These alarming moves in the White House set a dangerous precedent for the future of reproductive rights, putting us one step closer to a nationwide abortion ban. I, for one, am not about to stand idly by while these anti-choice policies wreak havoc on our nation. I am prepared to do everything in my power to fight for our bodily autonomy, but I need to know where you stand. I know just how passionate you are about protecting our most fundamental freedoms, friend. That's why I've personally selected you to represent your area in my urgent reproductive rights poll. | | Do you support reproductive rights? DEADLINE : Midnight | | | | Thank you. Jeanne Shaheen | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. To keep up with the latest, follow us on , and . name: Bill Gray
subject: A tough question
body:
_**A message from our sponsor...**_

| | | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=4ce0e0248e2142bca7df07261d3161ef&_e=EtyI9rpK0TNfCuUjXnDxhuh2hw7ZdZA7TwoYheMMX1gk9YIYo4IVD9OZaVa7qmC30Z-xLlN_x0O40XP1dsGqCLtZzNadTZKbhtAoENXakiM5JduKbMF5DSxrXHx77wcTflMmwE5O9BqPqLxi56sl-TcZK-3iP01j9EWyP1IcfIbh3BgjaxIulyZPOXbbfDtLr1PmkZ_Ufm-6yw5R6yTM2ZaJUafuZjBmHs6ERpgRgHescg-G-IRXQiadoGXLR5FnZFtxZ0rumPw2SRJk5pa_e_6yhnDdY1noyUH7KveWEakQoW1UrGg13att5mMGzLF2sU99V6q8q7s_hcyagRG-nzSw6FAZXdNLJsdnCTj_-c0z-SRROVO80LSardNnmSKTZJ2ha9piP2s3oDWshVhSbg%3D%3D)
---
| | Peter,

There's a tough question that I have to ask you on this fine day.

Here it is...What is true about the world?

Tough, right?

It's a question mankind has asked since ancient times--since creation itself. So, if you don't know the answer, that's fine (if you do, email me because I would love to know more!).

This question is especially pressing today because of the growing power modern humans have to change and manipulate the material world.

That's one of the reasons we released our free online course, **" The Great Principles of Chemistry."**

Why chemistry?

For one, this course brings a core subject studied at Hillsdale College to millions of Americans, including you.

More fundamentally, we study chemistry as part of our liberal arts education at Hillsdale because the sciences help us understand important aspects of reality. It helps us think about our role as humans in the world around us and reveals the framework, implications, and potential for human life.

Like all our courses, **" The Great Principles of Chemistry"** contains stunning visuals, study guides, quizzes, and in this case, hands-on experiments. This content is especially well-suited for science teachers, homeschool parents, or lifelong learners.

And our online courses are free. That's why we depend on the generosity of people like you to help us share the course with as many people as possible.

Would you consider making a tax-deductible donation to help promote this course?

**For any donation of $100 or more, you will receive a beautiful DVD box set for "The Great Principles of Chemistry," by clicking the link below:

**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/principles-of-chemistry](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=4ce0e0248e2142bca7df07261d3161ef&_e=EtyI9rpK0TNfCuUjXnDxhuh2hw7ZdZA7TwoYheMMX1gk9YIYo4IVD9OZaVa7qmC30Z-xLlN_x0O40XP1dsGqCLtZzNadTZKbhtAoENXakiM5JduKbMF5DSxrXHx77wcTflMmwE5O9BqPqLxi56sl-TcZK-3iP01j9EWyP1IcfIbh3BgjaxIulyZPOXbbfDtLr1PmkZ_Ufm-6yw5R6yTM2ZaJUafuZjBmHs6ERpgRgHescg-G-IRXQiadoGXLR5Fnp0079SkQ77XJ9HzfLEDQpjBKbNUTkVy0v6R6SycIC2X2j_cluLPBWMS5knWJGbxGErG82dOJ7NnjtSN7_N9ZBNqOU19IqMOZ_eVt5n5Jg-6-dkkBQLCr2ISLzL828lPh8fLFblbH3pgAwjWFo9DooQ%3D%3D)

The proper study of chemistry helps us understand the world and inspires wonder and awe at the beauty of creation and its Creator.

For these reasons, please join us in helping others gain a greater appreciation for the world around us with **" The Great Principles of Chemistry."**

Let's promote what's true about our world together.

Warm Regards,

Bill Gray, ‘01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

[Unsubscribe](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_s=4ce0e0248e2142bca7df07261d3161ef&_t=8f8ghiSj993Fz8AQPnfWFMGEpEVWaL4C3yYzQUF8ID6-LNrb6YvAq6yDVlftnj2H97oeCtCqdCbk7dAgFrZtndauyzT25_AyjoMeKkjHZG-J5fbvI0ZpNRPoL4iNfU2P)

---

name: Gay Valimont
subject: I’m the Democrat running to REPLACE Matt Gaetz
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
|
---
Hi team, I’m Gay Valimont! I’m a mom, a community advocate, and the Democrat running to REPLACE Matt Gaetz and deliver a CRUSHING blow to MAGA Extremism in Congress. [**Before I go any further, can I count on you to help me WIN this special election and flip this seat into Democrat hands by donating $10, or whatever you can afford -- split evenly with Seth Moulton?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101898657/527423826/1131448904?refcode=250210_MFC_Gay_Replacement&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZWQ0Y2M5Zi1lZmU3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hBDWojFBGCp90eifd11bx85X7t4eiamUVx6TsnbjepE=) [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101898659/527423828/1131448904?refcode=250210_MFC_Gay_Replacement&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZWQ0Y2M5Zi1lZmU3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hBDWojFBGCp90eifd11bx85X7t4eiamUVx6TsnbjepE=) For far too long, this district has been at the center of controversy and scandal. Matt Gaetz’s name has become synonymous with everything wrong in Congress. All Gaetz has brought back to the district chaos, extremism, and self-interest, leaving the people of our great district DESPERATE for real change. Together, we have the chance to do something extraordinary. We can turn the page and send a leader to Congress who actually represents you. Someone who believes in listening to the people, not just pandering to the MAGA base. Here’s why I’m running: ▶️To bring integrity back to Congress: I believe public service should mean serving the public – not personal ambition. ▶️ To protect our democracy: Our democracy has been under attack, and it’s time to fight back against extremism. ▶️To deliver for Florida families: Affordable healthcare, good jobs, and safe schools shouldn’t just be campaign promises – they should be reality. I’m not a politician chasing the next headline. And, like you, I’ve had enough of the noise and chaos in Congress. My ONLY focus is to make life better for working-class people. This is a special election, and the stakes couldn’t be higher. With the GOP holding only a slim majority in the House, flipping this seat would send a clear message: MAGA extremism isn’t what America stands for. But here’s the truth: winning this race will be an uphill battle. The GOP is already pouring resources into keeping this seat under their control. That’s why I’m asking for your help today. [**Can I count on you to chip in $25, $50, or $100 -- split with Seth Moulton -- to become a Founding Donor and help us fight back against MAGA Extremism?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101898662/527423830/1131448904?refcode=250210_MFC_Gay_Replacement&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZWQ0Y2M5Zi1lZmU3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hBDWojFBGCp90eifd11bx85X7t4eiamUVx6TsnbjepE=) [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101898664/527423833/1131448904?refcode=250210_MFC_Gay_Replacement&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZWQ0Y2M5Zi1lZmU3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hBDWojFBGCp90eifd11bx85X7t4eiamUVx6TsnbjepE=) This is our chance to bring new leadership to Northwest Florida and send a message loud and clear: Congress is no place for chaos. It’s time to focus on solutions, not scandals. Whether it’s $10, $100, or whatever you can afford, every single donation gets us closer to victory on April 1st. And trust me, the only April Fools we’ll celebrate are the MAGA extremists who underestimate us. [_**Chip in now -- split with Seth Moulton -- to help replace Matt Gaetz with a leader who will fight for you!**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101898665/527423835/1131448904?refcode=250210_MFC_Gay_Replacement&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZWQ0Y2M5Zi1lZmU3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hBDWojFBGCp90eifd11bx85X7t4eiamUVx6TsnbjepE=) Thank you for believing in this campaign. Together, we’re going to build something amazing – and it starts with you. Let’s get to work. With hope,
Gay Valimont
Democrat for Florida’s 1st Congressional District [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101898666/527423837/1131448904?refcode=250210_MFC_Gay_Replacement&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZWQ0Y2M5Zi1lZmU3LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hBDWojFBGCp90eifd11bx85X7t4eiamUVx6TsnbjepE=)
